marketState: neutral-to-bullish — extreme cooling demand offsetting a bearish storage surplus and a low-reliability seasonal fade signal
summary: Weather is the standout signal this run: demand is running +39.7% above the 5-year norm over 1-7D and +32.8% over 8-16D, with both readings above the top of their typical ranges and still rising (+2.1 24h). Fair value is drifting up with that demand (now $2.960 to +21D $3.128) while the front ($3.255) sits only slightly above the band, not stretched, and price is running +2.6pp stronger than the seasonal median. That's countered by a storage surplus of +151 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish weekly surprise, and a seasonality model that favors a fade over the next month (only 19% odds of being higher in 4 weeks, typical move -1.8%) — though that seasonal read carries Low reliability (51pp spread). Managed money is unusually net short (18th 5y percentile), a crowded position that could fuel a squeeze if heat persists. Net effect: a cautious long bias driven by the weather/fair-value trajectory, sized modestly because seasonality and storage argue the other way.

longTerm.rationale: Both weather windows show demand above the top of their 5y ranges and still climbing, pulling the fair-value estimate up from $2.960 today to $3.128 by +21D — price is only slightly over that rising band, not extended. A deeply net-short managed-money position (18th percentile) adds squeeze risk on top of the heat catalyst.
longTerm.invalidation: Demand forecasts revert toward/below the 5y average, the fair-value trajectory stalls or turns lower, or storage keeps printing bearish surprises that drag the band down through the stop.

shortTerm.rationale: Near-term demand is +39.7% above 5y and above the top of its range, 15m/4h technicals are bullish with 50>200 EMA crosses and positive MACD, and the front contract trades at a premium to Sep (backwardation), signaling prompt tightness. Price is only slightly over fair value ($3.255 vs $2.960), leaving room before it looks stretched.
shortTerm.invalidation: 1-7D demand vs 5y drops back toward normal, front loses its premium to Sep (curve flips to contango), or 4h/1d trend flips bearish with MACD turning negative.

keyDrivers: Cooling demand far above 5y norms in both windows (1-7D +39.7%, 8-16D +32.8%), both above their typical range max and still rising
Fair value trending higher ($2.960 now to $3.128 by +21D) while price stays only slightly over the band, not stretched
Managed money net short at just the 18th percentile of 5y range — a crowded short vulnerable to squeeze on further heat
Storage surplus +151 Bcf vs 5y with a bearish weekly surprise (+76 actual vs +70 forecast) caps upside
Seasonality favors a fade over the next month (19% odds higher at 4wk, typical -1.8% move) but reliability is Low (51pp spread)
risks: Seasonality reliability is Low (51pp 10-90% spread), so the seasonal fade signal could just be noise
Persistent storage surplus vs 5y is a structural bearish anchor if the heat wave breaks
Technicals are mixed across timeframes (1w trend bearish, 1h EMA 50<200), so longer-term momentum doesn't fully confirm the long thesis
